name: gpt-ai-markdown-editor
description: A full-featured WYSIWYG Markdown editor with visual table editing, drag-and-drop images, Mermaid diagrams, and distraction-free writing—all inside VS Code.

chatParticipants.name: markdown-editor
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions about your current markdown document

commands.title: Open with Flux Flow Markdown Editor
Flux Flow Markdown Editor: Get Selected Text

gptAiMarkdownEditor.mediaPath.description: Subfolder name for saved media files (images, attachments). Only used when Media Path Base is 'relativeToDocument' or 'workspaceFolder'.

enumDescriptions: Save media in a folder with the same name as the markdown file (e.g., my-doc/ next to my-doc.md). Recommended.
Save media in a subfolder relative to the current markdown file's directory.
Save media in a subfolder relative to the workspace root.
gptAiMarkdownEditor.mediaPathBase.description: Controls where media files (images, attachments) are saved.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.chromePath.markdownDescription:
Path to Google Chrome or Chromium executable for PDF export. Leave empty to auto-detect.

**Download Chrome:**
- [Windows/Mac/Linux](https://www.google.com/chrome/)
- [Chromium (open source)](https://www.chromium.org/getting-involved/download-chromium/)

**Common paths:**
- **Mac:** `/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome`
- **Windows:** `C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe`
- **Linux:** `/usr/bin/google-chrome` or `/usr/bin/chromium`

gptAiMarkdownEditor.pandocPath.markdownDescription:
Path to Pandoc executable for DOCX export. Leave empty to auto-detect.

**Install Pandoc:**
- [Download](https://pandoc.org/installing.html)

**Common paths:**
- **Mac:** `/usr/local/bin/pandoc` or `/opt/homebrew/bin/pandoc`
- **Windows:** `C:\Program Files\Pandoc\pandoc.exe`
- **Linux:** `/usr/bin/pandoc`

gptAiMarkdownEditor.pandocTemplatePath.markdownDescription: Optional path to a Pandoc template (.docx or .dotx) for DOCX export. If specified, Pandoc will use this template as the base for styling. Leave empty to use Pandoc's default template.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.customPromptsFile.markdownDescription: Optional path to a JSON file containing custom AI prompts. Used to power the inline 'Ask AI' and 'AI Summary' dropdown menus. Example format: `[{ "id": "score", "label": "Score Requirements", "prompt": "You are a Product Manager..." }]`.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.imageResize.skipWarn.description: Skip the warning dialog when resizing images. When enabled, images will be resized immediately without confirmation.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.themeOverride.description: Editor theme mode. Light or Dark — the editor uses its own color palette, independent of VS Code theme.

enumDescriptions: Open all markdown files with Flux Flow Markdown Editor by default
Open all markdown files with VS Code's default markdown preview
Ask me each time (shows prompt on first markdown file)
gptAiMarkdownEditor.defaultMarkdownViewe.description: Default markdown viewer preference when opening markdown files.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.tocMaxDepth.description: Maximum heading depth shown in the Table of Contents pane (1 = H1 only, 3 = H1–H3, 6 = all levels).

gptAiMarkdownEditor.preserveHtmlComments.markdownDescription: **Preserve HTML Comments**: When enabled, HTML comments (`<!-- ... -->`) in markdown files are retained during editing and round-trip on save. Comments are shown as subtle badges in the editor.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.editorZoomLevel.description: Editor zoom level (0.7 = 70%, 1.0 = 100%, 1.5 = 150%). Persisted across all documents.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.editorWidth.description: Maximum content width in pixels for the editor (800–2560px). At narrower viewport widths, content will be narrower than this maximum.

enumDescriptions: GPT-4.1 — Best quality, recommended for most tasks
GPT-4.1 Mini — Fast and capable, good balance of speed and quality
gptAiMarkdownEditor.aiModel.markdownDescription: **AI Model**: The language model used for AI text refinement (Rephrase, Shorten, Summarize, etc.). All models are free with GitHub Copilot.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.llmProvider.description: Select the AI provider used for text and image generation features.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.ollamaEndpoint.description: The Ollama server endpoint used for Ollama model calls.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.ollamaModel.description: The Ollama text model used for AI text operations when Ollama is selected.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.ollamaImageModel.description: The Ollama image model used for image creation when Ollama is selected.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.embed.description: The embedding model used for knowledge graph indexing and search.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.rag.t.description: Number of knowledge graph results used for retrieval-augmented generation.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.rag.c.description: The number of characters to include from each document when building RAG context.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.rag.f.description: Number of tokens used when generating full-text search snippets.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.rag.h.description: The number of RAG history turns to keep for retrieval-augmented generation.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.showSelectionToolbar.markdownDescription: **Selection Toolbar (Beta)**: Show a floating formatting toolbar when text is selected. Provides quick access to Bold, Italic, Highlight, and other formatting options.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.enabl.markdownDescription: **Knowledge Graph (Experimental)**: Enable wiki-link indexing, backlinks panel, and full-text search across all markdown files in the workspace. Requires reload after changing.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.knowledgeGraph.dataD.markdownDescription: Custom directory for Knowledge Graph data. Defaults to `~/.fluxflow`. Data persists across extension reinstalls.

gptAiMarkdownEditor.developerMode.markdownDescription: **Developer Mode**: Show detailed runtime errors in-editor and keep diagnostic logging enabled for troubleshooting.

scripts.build:debug: npm run test:settings && npm run build:extension:debug && npm run build:webview:debug
scripts.build:release: npm run test:settings && npm run build:extension:release && npm run build:webview:release && npm run test:roundtrip && npm run verify-build
scripts.precommit: npm run lint && npm run test
scripts.validate: npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build:debug
scripts.vsix: npm run build:release && npm run prepackage:release && npm run package:release
